clues: Classic symptoms of this disease were recorded in ancient Mesopotamia, interpreted as divine punishment where gods allowed ghosts of their enemies to haunt the affected individuals

Did you know? The original definition of nostalgia was not sentimental longing. The term was actually coined in the 17th century to describe this severe, potentially fatal illness in Swiss mercenaries which was attributed to homesickness
Modern understanding of the disorder was largely shaped by Vietnam veterans and advocacy groups in the 1970s where it ultimately became a recognized diagnosis in 1980 in the DSM-III
Ancient Greek and Egyptian artwork frequently depicted the god Harpocrates, the god of silence, with physical signs matching this disease

This disease was previously known as Little's disease due the the groundbreaking research in the 1860s by an orthopedic surgeon Dr. Little into understanding the cause of the muscle spasticity and early surgical interventions
Later proven correct, Dr. Sigmund Freud proposed the radical theory that this incurable condition originated from abnormal fetal development rather than just traumatic birth injuries.

Keep your distance. During World War I, devastating outbreaks caused by this hit military camps where recruits lived in close, crowded barracks. In 1918, Captain Glover published a revolutionary paper showing that simply spacing recruits' beds at least 2.5 feet apart drastically reduced the transmission

Because the mortality rate of this disease reached nearly 100% in early 19th-century epidemics, doctors resorted to desperate, dangerous methods. Patients were subjected to bloodletting, consumption of alcohol and opium, topical/oral mercury, and even puncturing the subarachnoid space to drain cerebrospinal fluid

Unlike other bacteria, this has an endotoxin in the outer membrane. When they rapidly multiply and die, they release this endotoxin which damages blood vessels and causes the signature purple-red rashes
Ancient Greeks and Romans believed this body part was the source of human spirit, heat, and complex emotions
Greek mythology features Prometheus having this organ eaten by a vulture, only to have it regrow each night. This myth accurately reflects the regenerative properties of this organ

This organ holds about 13% of your body's blood supply, filtering approximately 1.7 liters of blood every minute. While typically about 2-3 pounds, the largest recorded measurement in a human was 20.5 pounds
The chemical structure of this is remarkably similar to methamphetamine and is the only synthetic cathinone approved by the FDA, the same chemical family as bath salts
This medication lowers the seizure threshold so doses were capped and most people take slow-release formulas to avoid peak plasma concentrations
Approved prior to SSRIs, this antidepressant medication inhibits both norepinephrine and dopamine, but lacks serotonergic acivity.
